snippets.title: PR-template skeleton (`.github/pull_request_template.md`)
snippets.code:
## What
<1-2 sentences describing the change>

## Why
Fixes #<issue>. Context: <link to design doc / Slack thread / eval report>.

## How
<Non-obvious design choices, alternatives considered, trade-offs taken>

## Test plan
- [ ] Unit tests added / updated
- [ ] Integration / eval run: <paste result or link>
- [ ] Manual smoke check on staging: <describe>

## Screenshots / outputs (if applicable)
<paste here>
snippets.explanation: Save as `.github/pull_request_template.md` — GitHub auto-populates the PR body with this on every new PR.

snippets.title: Open a PR from the CLI (`gh`)
snippets.code:
# Install GitHub CLI: brew install gh / pacman -S github-cli / etc.
gh auth login

# From your feature branch:
gh pr create \
  --title "Tighten QA system prompt to reduce hallucinations" \
  --body-file PR_NOTES.md \
  --base main \
  --reviewer alice,bob

# Mark as draft:
gh pr create --draft ...

# Check the PR's CI status without leaving the terminal:
gh pr checks
snippets.explanation: Saves the round-trip through the browser; great for muscle memory once you've used the web UI enough to understand the model.

snippets.title: GitHub branch-protection rule (rough JSON via API)
snippets.explanation: Settings → Branches → Edit rule for `main`. Most teams configure this in the web UI; the API/JSON form lives in IaC repos (Terraform's `github_branch_protection`).

snippets.title: CODEOWNERS — auto-route reviewers
snippets.explanation: GitHub/GitLab auto-request reviews from the listed owners when a PR touches matching paths. Combine with 'require code owner review' branch protection for enforcement.
